concept: The Depth Model — a cross-section of the Gulf of Alaska water column where the hero is the probability instrument itself: a glowing depth-occupancy band that breathes with the day/night cycle, built from 13 years of Chinook salmon tagging data, that a trawl net reads to dodge accidental king-salmon bycatch.
references: Kurzgesagt's layered, backlit underwater/cross-section science explainers (depth, parallax, glow)
Bloomberg Businessweek data-journalism cross-sections (editorial-schematic clarity over a real physical slice)

taste_dials.notes: A quieter, more meditative story than the last two (a grid ledger, a ten-minute drone race), so motion intensity sits at 5, a breathing tide not a race. Density stays moderate-high (6) because the depth axis, probability band, and diel dial are all load-bearing data, but negative space around the water keeps it from feeling cluttered. Color boldness at 5: a restrained indigo-navy ocean world with one bold bioluminescent gold accent, not full saturation.

palette: abyssal indigo-navy water column, a bioluminescent gold band, moonlit silver for the night pass, one coral-dawn beat at the diel turn
metaphor: the depth model breathes: a probability tide rising and falling through day and night, and the net must find the one door it leaves open
honest_caveat: The model gives a population-level PROBABILITY of where Chinook sit by season and time of day, learned from 13 years of historical tagging, not a real-time tracker of any single fish. It cannot promise a given tow is clear, and no captain is required to use it. Drawn on screen as WHERE (the depth axis) staying sharp and gridded, while WHEN (this tow, today) frays into fog toward a ghosted question mark.
voice: kokoro:bf_emma (calm British, composed) — published; edge-tts en-GB-SoniaNeural used only as the draft timing reference, per config/voices.yaml workflow.
music_mood: contemplative, oceanic, slow-building with a hopeful turn — not glacial-ambient (already used for the beluga dispatch), not tense/dark.

fingerprint.palette: abyssal indigo-navy water column, a bioluminescent gold band, moonlit silver for the night pass, one coral-dawn beat at the diel turn
fingerprint.metaphor: the depth model breathes: a probability tide rising and falling through day and night, and the net must find the one door it leaves open

divergence_note: Unlike the data-brutalist parchment ledger of the grid/data-center story (07-02) or the orbital field-HUD countdown of the wildfire-drone story (07-03), this Dispatch slices SIDEWAYS into the water column itself: a stacked-layer cross-section whose hero is the depth model's own glowing probability readout, not a built structure or a swarm, moving as a scrolling time-of-day strip rather than an assemble-build or a radial countdown, in an indigo-and-gold ocean palette that is neither parchment nor spruce-black-and-ember.
timing_note: Beat/shot times below are ANCHORED TO THE REAL synthesized VO (out/dispatch/audio/timing60.json + words60.json), not hand-estimated seconds. Actual speech (Kokoro bf_emma, time-matched to an edge-tts en-GB-SoniaNeural timing reference) runs faster than the original estimate and ends at 48.23s, so the outro gets a generous ~11.8s tail, split into two further shots (6 and 7) so no single shot exceeds the 16s ceiling.

shots.centered: the SEASON CLOSED stamp, already mid-slam onto the surface-line gate at frame 1 (cold open on the consequence, not a slow establish)
shots.means: hooks on the consequence in the first second, then rewinds to explain 2024's two boats

shots.centered: one glowing Chinook silhouette, dead center, descending and fading before a river-mouth icon
shots.means: a real world-change lands well before 16s: policy becomes one fish's fate

shots.centered: the 700,000+ tag-point cloud coalescing, dead center in negative space, into one smooth curve
shots.means: the faded fish becomes the first data point; 13 years of tagging data assembles into the model

shots.centered: the assembled probability curve, now the glowing depth-occupancy band breathing with the day/night dial
shots.means: the model resolves back into the water column it describes, and the honest caveat plays: WHERE sharp, WHEN foggy

shots.centered: the trawl net's headrope depth line steering around the carried glowing risk band
shots.means: the model's output becomes a captain's actual choice of fishing depth, voluntary, not guaranteed

shots.centered: the wide Gulf of Alaska at dawn, boats visibly spaced clear of the glowing band, then the wordmark begins its reveal
shots.means: pulls back from one captain's choice to the whole fleet, run after run

shots.centered: the tagline and source credit, staged in over a quiet closing card, a genuinely different final composition, not a text overlay on shot 6's frame
shots.means: signs off on motion, never a static hold

beats.vo: A fishery can close over one bad weekend.
beats.shows: COLD OPEN: at frame 1 the SEASON CLOSED stamp is already mid-slam onto the surface-line gate; only after impact does the dusk cross-section settle into view around it
beats.sfx: hit (stamp slam at frame 1), then a settling boom
beats.means: hooks on the consequence in the first second, not after 8s

beats.vo: In twenty twenty four, two boats caught enough king salmon by accident
beats.shows: a fast reverse-wipe rewinds the frame to a '2024' date chip; two boat icons appear as the cause; a red tally ticks upward
beats.sfx: a fast rewind whoosh, then tick tick (tally counting)
beats.means: explains what caused the stamp we opened on

beats.vo: to shut down Alaska's pollock season.
beats.shows: the timeline catches back up to the present; gate/net icon fully shut, boats idle; shot 1 ends
beats.means: closes the causal loop before the first shot change

beats.vo: Every king salmon they take is one that never reaches a river.
beats.shows: match-cut to shot 2: now immersed IN the water at eye level, a single glowing Chinook silhouette descends and fades before a river-mouth icon
beats.means: reframes policy into one fish's fate; the real cut lands at 10.86s, well before the 16s ceiling

beats.vo: So a University of Alaska Fairbanks team tried something new.
beats.shows: the faded fish dissolves to a single point of light, still inside shot 2's immersive void
beats.sfx: a soft tick as it fades to a point
beats.means: the individual loss becomes the first data point, seeding shot 3

beats.vo: Thirteen years of tagged salmon in the Gulf of Alaska,
beats.shows: assemble-cut to shot 3: '13 YEARS · GULF OF ALASKA' label locks in at the exact screen position the point occupied; more tag-points bloom into the void
beats.means: establishes dataset scale, carrying the point from shot 2

beats.vo: over seven hundred thousand data points,
beats.shows: hundreds more points bloom rapidly, each landing at its own depth/time coordinate in the void, not a generic counter
beats.means: the scale of the evidence, shown rather than counted

beats.vo: trained a model that predicts where kings sit in the water
beats.shows: the point-cloud visibly collapses inward and resolves into one smooth glowing probability curve
beats.sfx: a rising riser into a whoosh (assemble payoff), a resolving pulse
beats.means: the BUILD payoff: noise becomes a model; shot 3 ends

beats.vo: by season and time of day.
beats.shows: graphic-match cut to shot 4: the curve resolves into the water column's glowing depth-occupancy band; a day/night dial ticks beside it
beats.means: the model returns to the sea it describes; the film's home composition returns

beats.vo: It cannot track one fish, or promise one tow comes up clear.
beats.shows: the band stays sharp and gridded along the DEPTH axis (WHERE); toward a 'this tow, today' readout it visibly frays and is EATEN by fog around a ghosted '?'
beats.sfx: a thin riser fraying into soft noise, a faint tick on the '?'
beats.means: draws the one honest caveat as a cost to the model, not a decoration; shot 4 ends

beats.vo: What it shows is the odds, so a captain can choose a depth
beats.shows: mask-wipe carries the glowing band into shot 5: a trawl net's headrope depth line steers around it; a 'PROPOSED, NOT GUARANTEED' ghosted label
beats.sfx: whoosh (cut to boat), mechanical creak (net paying out)
beats.means: the model becomes an actual, voluntary choice at sea

beats.shows: the net settles at the chosen depth: pollock visibly fill it while the Chinook's glow stays clear above, a shown outcome
beats.sfx: hit (net catch), a soft resolve tone
beats.means: shows the payoff rather than a repeated text chip

beats.shows: a plain 'VOLUNTARY' stamp lands, visibly and audibly softer than the SEASON CLOSED stamp; shot 5 ends
beats.means: the honest note on adoption, rhymed against the opening stamp

beats.vo: But if enough boats do, the same fish and the same nets could add up to a better bet,
beats.shows: map-travel cut to shot 6: wide dawn view of the Gulf of Alaska, several boats visibly spaced clear of the glowing band
beats.means: pulls back from one captain to the whole fleet

beats.shows: the spaced boats settle into a calm, steady dawn pattern across the gulf
beats.means: lands the final words visually before the outro begins

beats.shows: the ALASKA.AI wordmark begins its reveal over the still-drifting aerial (parallax continues, camera never freezes); shot 6 ends
beats.means: the branded sign-off begins; motion never stops

beats.shows: crossfade to shot 7: a quiet closing card (single-object-void); the tagline and source credit reveal in staged beats
beats.sfx: pop (tagline lands), a soft tick (credit line)
beats.means: a genuinely different, calmer final composition for the sign-off, not a text overlay on shot 6's frame

beats.shows: a gentle cinematic fade to black, the drift/parallax still moving into the very last frame
beats.sfx: a gentle resolve chime, fading with the picture
beats.means: ends on motion, never a static hold
